digest.component_or_area: plan-review verification impact model (plan-v2 to plan-v3)
propositions: The grade-blind 0.85 hard floor on ac_unverifiable auto-blocks any non-none grade equally, making refinement-demand findings block with the same force as genuinely untestable criteria.

The ac_unverifiable axis is split into three oracle-kind grades: missing_oracle and broken_oracle keep the 0.85 hard floor, while underspecified_oracle scores <=0.55, below the 0.60 lowest blocking threshold, so it surfaces and coaches but never auto-blocks.
Write-time enforcement changes PlanSeverityAttrs.ac_unverifiable to a closed Literal grade set so out-of-vocabulary grades are rejected at verification-parse time.
enrich_operator_attested clears missing_oracle and underspecified_oracle to none on attested evidence but never clears broken_oracle.
IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bumps plan-v2 to plan-v3 while the sidecar schema id stays plan_review_result_v2; legacy plan-v2 records are read as-is via version segmentation for back-compat.

Both single-turn and agentic verifier prompts define the three grades with one worked example each, and the change is validated by new unit tests including rejection, persistence, enrich-asymmetry, and legacy round-trip pins.
